row.title: Chicken Stroganoff
row.raw_ingredients: 2 1/2 cups reduced sodium chicken stock (divided); 1 Tbsp all purpose flour; 3 Tbsp olive oil (divided); 1 lb. chicken breasts, cut into approximately 1 inch pieces; 1 tsp kosher salt (divided); 3/4 tsp black pepper, (divided); 12 oz cremini mushrooms, sliced; 1/2 yellow onion, finely chopped; 1/2 tsp paprika; 1/4 tsp dried thyme; 1/4 tsp dried oregano; 1/8 tsp red pepper flakes; 3-4 cloves garlic, minced; 1/2 cup dry white wine (I used a chardonnay); 6 oz uncooked extra wide egg noodles; 3 - 4 Tbsp sour cream (more can be used if you want); 1 tsp whole-grain or dijon mustard; chopped fresh parsley, (for garnish); extra black pepper, for garnish
row.expected_swaps: egg noodles>egg-white noodles, veggie noodles, or legume noodles; sour cream>greek yogurt
row.notes: Restrained for a stroganoff - reduced-sodium stock, only 3-4 tbsp dairy. Noodle swap now names real alternatives: egg-WHITE noodles (mostly egg, high protein) or legume/veggie noodles - not the refined wheat egg noodles the dish starts with (that circular listing is fixed).

concerns.why: trivial thickener
cooks off, flavor only

swaps.to_ingredient: egg-white noodles, veggie noodles, or legume noodles
swaps.reason: less processed + higher protein - regular egg noodles are refined wheat; egg-white noodles are mostly egg, and legume/veggie noodles cut the refined grain entirely
less processed + healthier - same tang, more protein

review.comment: it has egg noodles as an alternative to egg noodles... I also dont know - are egg noodles healthy? When I put them down as an alternative i meant like egg white noodles that i believe are just made of egg
review.superseded_expected_swaps: egg noodles>homemade pasta, or veggie noodles, or bean/egg noodles; sour cream>greek yogurt
review.superseded_notes: Restrained for a stroganoff - reduced-sodium stock, only 3-4 tbsp dairy. Pasta ladder applied to the noodles.

row.title: Instant Pot Coconut Tandoori-Inspired Chicken
row.raw_ingredients: 2 pounds boneless skinless chicken thighs; 2 tablespoons honey; 2 tablespoons garam masala; 1 tablespoon chili powder; 1 tablespoon garlic powder; 1 tablespoon cumin; 2 teaspoons salt; 2 teaspoons turmeric; 1/2 teaspoon ground ginger; 1/2 teaspoon cayenne pepper; 14-ounce can coconut milk; 2 cups rice, uncooked
row.expected_swaps: white rice (2 cups)>brown rice; canned coconut milk>coconut milk that's just coconut + water (no gums/stabilizers)
row.notes: Chicken thighs + a real spice cabinet. Honey rule applied - target 72->73; white rice is the main drag. Coconut milk elevated from a note to a real sourcing swap per your point that picking the right can matters (read the label for gums/stabilizers).

concerns.why: modest, and an acceptable unrefined sweetener

swaps.reason: less processed - whole grain under the same sauce

swaps.to_ingredient: coconut milk that's just coconut + water (no gums/stabilizers)
swaps.reason: better sourcing - many canned brands add gums, emulsifiers, and preservatives; read the label

review.comment: picking the right canned coconut milk it important
review.superseded_notes: Chicken thighs + a real spice cabinet. Honey rule applied - target 72->73; white rice is the main drag.

row.title: Broccoli Cheddar Soup
row.raw_ingredients: 4 tablespoons unsalted butter; 1 medium yellow onion, chopped; ½ teaspoon sea salt; Freshly ground black pepper; 3 garlic cloves, chopped; ¼ cup all-purpose flour; 2 cups whole milk or unsweetened almond milk; 2 cups vegetable broth; 3 cups chopped broccoli florets; 1 large carrot, julienned or finely chopped; ½ teaspoon Dijon mustard; 8 ounces shredded cheddar cheese, about 2 heaping cups; Homemade croutons, for serving, optional
row.expected_swaps: shredded cheddar (8 oz)>block sharp cheddar, grated fresh; all-purpose flour roux>blended potato or white beans to thicken; vegetable broth>low-sodium vegetable broth; croutons>homemade croutons from real bread
row.notes: Vegetable soup by name, cheese sauce by construction - the cheese is handled as sourcing, not villainized. Target 64->66. Your two QA catches fixed: vegetable broth is now flagged (it's the sodium the low-sodium swap targets), and butter is addressed - most is the roux the potato/bean swap removes, the remainder is aromatics (olive oil).

concerns.why: most of it is the roux, which the potato/bean thickener removes, the rest sautes the aromatics

swaps.reason: better sourcing - bagged shreds carry anticaking agents; sharp gives the same flavor from less

swaps.to_ingredient: blended potato or white beans to thicken
swaps.reason: less processed - body from actual food (this also removes most of the butter)
healthier - the sodium the swap targets
less processed - homemade counts

review.comment: veggie broth didnt get flagged but it is in the swap... butter got flagged but not in the swap
review.superseded_expected_swaps: shredded cheddar>block sharp cheddar grated fresh; flour roux>blended potato or white beans; broth>low-sodium; croutons>homemade from real bread
review.superseded_notes: Vegetable soup by name, cheese sauce by construction - but per your rule the cheese is handled as sourcing, not villainized. Target 64->66.

row.title: Hot Spinach-Artichoke Dip
row.raw_ingredients: Cooking spray; 1/2 cup (120ml) mayonnaise; 1/2 cup (120ml) sour cream; 1 tablespoon Dijon mustard; 1 tablespoon white miso, optional; 2 teaspoons grated lemon zest (from 1 lemon); 1 medium clove garlic (5g), minced; 8 ounces (226g) plain, full-fat cream cheese, cut into 8 pieces and softened; 15 ounces (425g) frozen cut-leaf spinach, thawed and squeezed dry (about 1 1/4 cups); One 14-ounce (397g) can quartered artichoke hearts, rinsed, drained, patted dry, and coarsely chopped (about 1 1/2 cups); 12 ounces (340g) Monterey Jack cheese or mild white cheddar cheese, shredded (about 3 cups), divided; 4 ounces (115g) Parmigiano-Reggiano cheese, grated (about 2 heaping cups), divided; Crackers, pita chips, or crudités, for serving
row.expected_swaps: mayonnaise>homemade mayo or greek yogurt; cream cheese>blended cottage cheese; shredded Monterey Jack (12 oz)>block jack, grated fresh; crackers / pita chips>crudites; cooking spray>olive oil brush
row.notes: Real spinach and artichokes carrying a lot of combined dairy fat and mayo. Cheese treated as sourcing; the mayo + cream base is the true drag. Target 52->54. Cooking spray promoted from a note to a real swap (olive oil brush) per your call.

swaps.reason: less processed - homemade drops industrial oils; yogurt also cuts fat
less processed + healthier - same body, closer to plain curd
better sourcing - bagged shreds carry anticaking agents
less processed - the vehicle is half the dish in practice
less processed - drops propellants and emulsifiers for one real ingredient

review.superseded_expected_swaps: mayonnaise>homemade mayo or greek yogurt; cream cheese>blended cottage cheese; shredded jack>block jack grated fresh; crackers>crudites
review.superseded_notes: Real spinach and artichokes carrying a lot of combined dairy fat and mayo. Cheese treated as sourcing; the mayo + cream base is the true drag. Target 52->54.

row.title: Chicken Gyro Bowls
row.raw_ingredients: 1 lb chicken breasts, cubed; 3 Tbsp lemon juice; 1 Tbsp olive oil; 1 tsp paprika; 1 tsp dried oregano; ½ tsp dried basil; ½ tsp cumin; ½ tsp salt; ½ tsp pepper; ½ cup plain greek yogurt (or sour cream); 1 Tbsp fresh lemon juice; ½ tsp salt; 1 tsp garlic; ¼ cup cucumber, chopped; 2 Tbsp fresh dill; 1 Tbsp fresh cilantro; 3 Tbsp feta (optional! ); ¼ cup tomatoes, chopped; ¼ cup cucumber, diced; ½ cup cooked quinoa; ¼ cup kalamata olives; shredded romaine lettuce; 3 Tbsp red onions, diced; feta, crumbled
row.notes: Whole-food bowl: lean chicken, veg, quinoa, yogurt sauce. Feta + olives add sodium but only in garnish amounts. No swaps needed.

row.title: Sweet Potato Beef Cottage Cheese Bowl

row.title: One Pot Lemon-Rosemary Chicken
row.raw_ingredients: 4 skinless, boneless chicken breasts; 3/4 pound small red-skinned potatoes, halved; 2 sprigs fresh rosemary, plus 1 Tbsp leaves; 1 clove garlic, smashed; Pinch of red pepper flakes; Juice of 2 lemons (squeezed halves reserved); 1 Tbsp extra-virgin olive oil; 2 tsp. sea salt
row.notes: Textbook clean: eight whole ingredients, olive oil, fresh herbs. Only knob is the 2 tsp salt. Good top-of-scale anchor.

row.title: Raspberry Lemon Yogurt Bread
row.raw_ingredients: 1 cup whole wheat pastry flour; 3/4 cup all-purpose flour; 1 tsp baking powder; 1/2 tsp baking soda; 1/2 tsp salt; 3/4 cup plain Greek yogurt; 2 eggs (room temperature); 1/4 cup butter (melted and cooled); 3/4 cup cane sugar; 1/4 cup fresh lemon juice + zest of 1 lemon; 2 tsp vanilla extract; 1 cup fresh raspberries; 1/2 cup powdered sugar; 1 Tbsp fresh lemon juice
row.expected_swaps: cane sugar>honey (about 1/2 cup); all-purpose flour>white whole wheat flour; powdered sugar glaze>greek yogurt-lemon drizzle
row.notes:
Homemade quick bread with real yogurt and berries, but ~1 cup total added sugar makes it a dessert; the sugar dimension dominates. Yes - cane sugar swaps cleanly to honey here. With the honey + drizzle swaps it climbs toward Mostly Clean.

Greek yogurt lemon drizzle doesnt have an explanation for how to make it or where it comes from

concerns.why: structural in a quick bread

swaps.reason: less processed - honey is an unrefined sweetener and yogurt quick breads take it well; drop oven temp ~25F
less processed - keeps the grain intact without changing texture much
less processed + healthier - replaces a pure refined-sugar topping with real food

row.title: Keto chilli con carne

Whole-food chilli; the deliberate keto fat load drags fat quality, and how hard to punish intentional fat is the ambiguity. Cheese handled as sourcing (block, not pre-shredded) rather than punished.

Items of concern (suggestion is no swap):

row.title: The Best Queso Recipe
row.raw_ingredients: 2 Tablespoons unsalted butter; ⅓ cup finely diced white onion; 1 Tablespoon minced garlic; 4 oz can fire roasted diced green chilis (drained ); ¼ cup diced roma tomato or canned petite diced tomatoes (juices drained ); 2 teaspoons cornstarch; ¼ teaspoon ground cumin; ¼ teaspoon ancho chili powder; ⅛ teaspoon smoked paprika; Dash of cayenne pepper (for a spicier queso, optional); ¼ teaspoon ground black pepper; ¼ teaspoon table salt (plus additional to taste as needed); 12 oz can full-fat evaporated milk; 8 oz freshly grated sharp cheddar cheese (see note ); Corn chips (for serving)
row.expected_swaps: corn chips>baked tortilla wedges or veggie dippers; evaporated milk>whole milk + splash of cream
row.notes: From-scratch queso beats jarred, and the cheese is fresh-grated block already. Still a cheese-sauce-plus-chips dish. Target 50->52.

concerns.why: recipe already grates fresh from a block - right sourcing - and it IS the dish
alternatives: sharp cheddar for more flavor per ounce

concerns.why: fine as canned goods go

swaps.to_ingredient: baked tortilla wedges or veggie dippers
swaps.reason: less processed - the vehicle is half the dish
less processed - fresh dairy does the same job with the cornstarch

row.title: Pull-Apart Chopped Cheese
row.raw_ingredients: 1/4 cup mayonnaise; 1/4 medium onion, grated; 2 tablespoons ketchup; 2 teaspoons spicy brown mustard; 1 teaspoon pickle juice; 1/4 teaspoon sugar; 1/2 teaspoon sweet paprika; 1/2 teaspoon dried oregano; 1/4 teaspoon ground cumin; 1/4 teaspoon garlic powder; 1/4 teaspoon onion powder; 1/4 teaspoon ground coriander; Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper; One 12-ounce package Hawaiian dinner rolls (12 rolls); 2 tablespoons unsalted butter, melted; 3/4 medium onion, diced; 3 frozen beef patties (1 pound total); 8 ounces (2 cups) shredded Muenster cheese; 2 plum tomatoes, thinly sliced; 1/2 head iceberg lettuce, thinly sliced
row.expected_swaps: frozen beef patties>fresh 90% lean ground beef; Hawaiian rolls>homemade or bakery-fresh rolls; shredded Muenster>block Muenster sliced; ketchup + mayo sauce>homemade versions
row.notes: Frozen patties + sweet packaged rolls + a sugared mayo-ketchup sauce; the UPF carriers define it. Boundary with Processed.

swaps.reason: less processed - no patty binders or preservatives; names the fat content
less processed - packaged sweet rolls carry sweeteners and conditioners

swaps.to_ingredient: block Muenster from the cheese aisle, sliced
swaps.reason: better sourcing - avoids bagged-shred anticaking agents
less processed - homemade counts; also lets you skip the added 1/4 tsp sugar
